mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in real GDP growth expands corporate earnings expectations and reduces recession risk, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in GDP expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid 1-month increase in the federal funds rate exceeding 25 basis points signals aggressive tightening, which compresses equity valuation multiples and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day forward returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the rate hike signal.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent rises in the unemployment rate and declines in total nonfarm payrolls indicate deteriorating labor market conditions, reducing consumer spending power and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive or uncorrelated when both unemployment rises and payrolls decline simultaneously over the signal window.

mechanism_narrative: A rebound in the industrial production index from depressed levels signals supply chain restoration and capacity utilization recovery, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO momentum and 20-day forward returns falls below 0.04 over a 3-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: A collapse in housing starts below 600 thousand units reflects severe weakness in the construction sector and broader consumer confidence, depressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are not significantly negative when housing starts breach the 600 thousand threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated consumer price inflation combined with rising federal funds rates triggers stagflation expectations, compressing equity risk premiums and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive or show no statistical relationship when high inflation and rate hikes occur simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales and food services figures indicate robust consumer spending, which directly supports corporate revenue growth and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: Walk-forward correlation between RSAFS month-over-month changes and 5-day forward returns is negative or statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in the unemployment rate above 5.5 percent during an economic expansion signals labor market overheating and impending policy tightening, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Forward returns do not decline significantly when the unemployment rate breaches the 5.5 percent threshold during expansionary phases.
